title: Why Payment Solutions for Iranian Digital Sellers Is Costing You More Than You Think
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Selling digital products from Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, or Bangladesh is possible with crypto-native infrastructure that has no geographic restrictions.

content.text: Iranian digital sellers, be warned: getting stuck with a payment solution that costs more than you think is a costly mistake. You've built an online store, but PayPal, Stripe, and other major platforms are blocked, leaving you with limited options. You need a reliable payment solution that integrates with your existing store without the hassle of creating a new one. But if you're not careful, you might end up with a solution that eats into your profits, leaving you with less money in your pocket than you need to keep your business afloat.
The Mistake Almost Every Creator Makes First

Many Iranian digital sellers make the mistake of choosing payment solutions that are not optimized for their specific needs. They opt for cheap alternatives that promise the world but fail to deliver. These solutions often come with hidden fees, lengthy payment processing delays, and a lack of customer support, which can be a nightmare to deal with. As a result, these sellers end up losing money on each transaction, making it difficult to keep their businesses afloat. This is a common problem faced by many creators in countries with restricted payment systems.
Why This Keeps Happening to Good Stores

Good stores keep getting taken down by payment solutions that aren't designed to handle international transactions. These solutions often rely on outdated infrastructure that's not compatible with the complex payment systems used in countries like Iran. As a result, transactions are delayed, and fees are higher than expected, making it difficult for sellers to keep their businesses running smoothly. Moreover, these solutions often require sellers to create new accounts and go through lengthy verification processes, which can be time-consuming and frustrating.
The Real Cost of Getting This Wrong

The real cost of getting the payment solution wrong is loss of time, money, and revenue. When a payment solution fails to deliver, sellers are left with a huge headache trying to resolve the issue. They spend hours on the phone with customer support, filling out forms, and waiting for answers that never come. All this while, their customers are left wondering when their purchases will be fulfilled, leading to a loss of trust and revenue. In many cases, the cost of getting it wrong can be more than the revenue generated from the sale itself.
Getting the payment solution wrong can be more costly than the revenue generated from the sale itself.
How to Protect Yourself Before It Happens

To protect yourself from the costly mistake of getting the payment solution wrong, it's essential to do your homework. Research the payment solution thoroughly and read reviews from other sellers who have used the solution before. Look for solutions that are specifically designed for international transactions and have a good reputation for reliability and customer support. Web3 Wallet Connect, for instance, offers frontend wallet integration with multi-chain QR generation and live price feeds, allowing buyers to pay in 300+ coins. By choosing the right solution, you can avoid the headaches that come with getting it wrong.
Building the Store That Cannot Be Taken Down

Building a store that cannot be taken down requires a solid understanding of payment solutions and their limitations. It's not just about choosing a payment solution; it's about building a store that is designed to handle international transactions smoothly. This means investing in a solution that is specifically designed for your needs and has a good reputation for reliability and customer support. By building a store that cannot be taken down, you can avoid the costly mistake of getting the payment solution wrong and focus on what matters most – selling your digital products and growing your business.
